candidates.keyRisks: Petroleum price volatility, regulatory approvals, pipeline safety liabilities
Heritage building constraints, hospitality market competition
Negative equity, ongoing losses, bilateral air service agreement implications

transactions.notes: KenGen listed on NSE in 2006; government retains 70%
KPLC listed on NSE; government retains 50.1% through National Treasury

restructuringProjects.description: Comprehensive restructuring including route rationalisation, fleet right-sizing, cost reduction, and debt restructuring as conditions for KES 20 billion government recapitalisation
SGR debt restructuring negotiations with Exim Bank of China to extend repayment period from 20 to 30 years and reduce interest rate from 3.6% to 2.0%
KBC transformation from traditional broadcaster to digital media company, including OTT platform launch, content partnerships, and commercial revenue diversification

postSaleObligations.description: Maintain NSE listing, publish quarterly financial results, and comply with Capital Markets Authority disclosure requirements
postSaleObligations.lastAssessment: Full compliance with NSE listing rules maintained

postSaleObligations.description: Maintain electricity supply to all connected customers including rural and low-income consumers at regulated tariffs
postSaleObligations.lastAssessment: Service obligations met; lifeline tariff maintained
